codeReview.summary: The feature correctly retires the stale super-admin alias with a controlled `410 Gone` contract and adds fail-closed delivery handling, but the new two-phase reset-token issuance and cleanup sequence is not atomic and can invalidate every reset token when overlapping reset requests for the same user both succeed.

issues.description: `PasswordResetService.dispatchResetEmail(...)` now saves a new reset token in `issueResetToken(...)`, sends the email, and only then removes sibling tokens in `cleanupSupersededResetTokens(...)` using a second `REQUIRES_NEW` transaction. Two overlapping forgot/admin-reset requests for the same user can therefore cross-delete each other's tokens, leaving the user with delivered reset emails but no valid token row at all.
issues.evidence: In `PasswordResetService`, `issueResetToken(...)` persists the token before `cleanupSupersededResetTokens(...)` later executes `tokenRepository.deleteByUserAndIdNot(user, keepId)`. Because those steps are separated by email delivery and run in distinct transactions, there is no user-level lock or single last-writer-wins transaction protecting duplicate-submit or retry races. The updated `PasswordResetServiceTest`, `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT`, and `AuthControllerIT` cover delivery/configuration behavior but contain no concurrent/overlapping request coverage for this path.

summary: FAIL. Alias retirement and fail-closed delivery semantics landed, but the reset-token cleanup refactor introduces a race that can strand users without any valid reset token under overlapping requests.
